verses.text: Solomon reigns over all Israel and sets in place his chief officers — priests, scribes, recorders, the men who keep the kingdom running. A wise king does not rule alone but surrounds himself with capable, ordered servants. Good government is built on trustworthy people in their places.

Solomon reigns over all Israel and sets in place his chief officers — priests, scribes, recorders, the men who keep the kingdom running. A wise king does not rule alone but surrounds himself with capable, ordered servants. Good government is built on trustworthy people in their places.

Solomon reigns over all Israel and sets in place his chief officers — priests, scribes, recorders, the men who keep the kingdom running. A wise king does not rule alone but surrounds himself with capable, ordered servants. Good government is built on trustworthy people in their places.

More officials are named — over the army, the priesthood, the household, the labour. Each has his sphere; the work is divided and assigned. The LORD is a God of order, and a realm at peace reflects it: every task has its overseer, every duty its appointed hand.

More officials are named — over the army, the priesthood, the household, the labour. Each has his sphere; the work is divided and assigned. The LORD is a God of order, and a realm at peace reflects it: every task has its overseer, every duty its appointed hand.

More officials are named — over the army, the priesthood, the household, the labour. Each has his sphere; the work is divided and assigned. The LORD is a God of order, and a realm at peace reflects it: every task has its overseer, every duty its appointed hand.

Solomon appoints twelve officers, each responsible to provision the king's house one month a year. The burden is spread evenly across the land and the calendar. Wisdom shares a heavy load among many rather than crushing a few; order turns a vast need into a manageable rhythm.

The list of officers continues, region by region, covering the whole land from the valleys to the north. No corner of the kingdom is overlooked. Careful, comprehensive administration is itself a kind of faithfulness — making sure the work of the realm reaches everywhere.

The list of officers continues, region by region, covering the whole land from the valleys to the north. No corner of the kingdom is overlooked. Careful, comprehensive administration is itself a kind of faithfulness — making sure the work of the realm reaches everywhere.

The roll of provincial officers is completed, one over each district, with one over all the land. The structure is thorough and complete. A well-ordered kingdom does not happen by chance; it is the fruit of deliberate planning and steady oversight under a wise head.

Judah and Israel are as many as the sand by the sea, eating, drinking, and merry, and Solomon reigns from the Euphrates to Egypt's border. The promise to Abraham of countless seed is visibly fulfilled. God keeps His word; the little nation has become a great one.

Judah and Israel are as many as the sand by the sea, eating, drinking, and merry, and Solomon reigns from the Euphrates to Egypt's border. The promise to Abraham of countless seed is visibly fulfilled. God keeps His word; the little nation has become a great one.

Solomon's daily provision is vast — fine flour, meal, oxen, sheep, deer, and fatted fowl. The scale shows the prosperity of his reign. Yet such abundance is a stewardship as much as a blessing; much given is much to answer for, and plenty can soften as well as bless.

Solomon's daily provision is vast — fine flour, meal, oxen, sheep, deer, and fatted fowl. The scale shows the prosperity of his reign. Yet such abundance is a stewardship as much as a blessing; much given is much to answer for, and plenty can soften as well as bless.

He has peace on every side, and 'every man under his vine and under his fig tree' — the picture of settled, fearless rest. This is the golden age, a foretaste of the deeper peace the Prince of Peace will bring. Such rest is God's gift, and points beyond itself to Him.

He has peace on every side, and 'every man under his vine and under his fig tree' — the picture of settled, fearless rest. This is the golden age, a foretaste of the deeper peace the Prince of Peace will bring. Such rest is God's gift, and points beyond itself to Him.

Solomon gathers great numbers of horses and chariots, provisioned at his officers' charge. The splendour grows — yet here too is the quiet excess the law had warned a king against. Prosperity, even God-given, carries a hidden danger when it multiplies what God said to restrain.

Solomon gathers great numbers of horses and chariots, provisioned at his officers' charge. The splendour grows — yet here too is the quiet excess the law had warned a king against. Prosperity, even God-given, carries a hidden danger when it multiplies what God said to restrain.

God gives Solomon wisdom and understanding 'as the sand on the sea shore,' surpassing all the wise men of the East and Egypt; his fame spreads to every nation. The greatest gift is not his wealth but his God-given wisdom. What makes a person truly great is what God puts within.

Solomon speaks three thousand proverbs and a thousand and five songs, and discourses on trees, beasts, birds, and fish, so that people come from all kings to hear him. His wisdom embraced all of God's world. To know God's works rightly is part of the wisdom God gives His servants.
